title: Relative Valuation & Peer Comparison (Koyfin-style)

observation.gap_identified: No relative valuation vs sector peers — Koyfin's key differentiator (500+ screening metrics, peer comparison) was completely missing. Users had no way to see if a stock was cheap or expensive relative to its sector.

implementation.feature_built: Full relative valuation service: fetches 13 valuation metrics (P/E, Forward P/E, PEG, EV/EBITDA, P/S, P/B, dividend yield, FCF yield, revenue growth, earnings growth, profit margin, ROE, D/E) for a stock and all sector peers in parallel, computes percentile rankings within sector, generates composite valuation score (0-100), historical P/E context (current vs 5Y range), peer comparison table, and verdict (Deep Value / Undervalued / Fair Value / Overvalued / Significantly Overvalued). Lightweight summary wired into stock analysis response. Standalone endpoint for full peer comparison view.

limitations: Historical valuation approximates past P/E using current EPS (not quarterly EPS history) — imperfect for companies with volatile earnings
Peer group is limited to configured sector_stocks (5-23 per sector) — not the full GICS sector
FCF yield requires freeCashflow from yfinance which some tickers may not have
No EV/Revenue or EV/FCF metrics yet — could add in future cycle
assessment.self_critique: Could have also added a screener-level relative valuation column so the stock screener table shows valuation verdicts inline. The current implementation requires either the full stock analysis endpoint or the dedicated valuation endpoint. A screener enrichment would be higher visibility but also much slower (N*M yfinance calls).

next_steps: Add relative valuation verdict column to stock screener (batch-optimized, shared peer data)
Add EV/Revenue and EV/FCF metrics to the metric set
Build chart pattern recognition (TradingView gap — head/shoulders, triangles, flags)
Add tracking error analysis to portfolio (Bloomberg PORT gap)
Add style box classification (Morningstar gap — 3x3 size vs value/growth matrix)
